interactions.message: Paracetamol (Crocin/Dolo) can increase warfarin's blood-thinning effect and raise bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Do not combine without your doctor's advice. Watch for unusual bruising or bleeding.

interactions.message: Aspirin (Ecosprin) with warfarin greatly increases bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: This combination is usually avoided. Call your doctor immediately.

interactions.message: Aspirin with clopidogrel raises bleeding risk — sometimes prescribed together after a heart stent, but only under strict doctor supervision.
interactions.recommendation: Confirm with your cardiologist before taking both.

interactions.message: Pantoprazole (Pan-D) may reduce methotrexate clearance and increase side effects.
interactions.recommendation: Tell your doctor if you take both. They may adjust timing or dose.

interactions.message: Some PPIs like pantoprazole may slightly reduce clopidogrel's anti-clotting effect.
interactions.recommendation: Ask your doctor if you need both — do not stop either medicine on your own.

interactions.message: Telmisartan (Telma) with potassium supplements may cause dangerously high potassium.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid potassium supplements unless prescribed. Call your doctor if you feel weak or have an irregular heartbeat.

interactions.message: Losartan with potassium supplements can raise potassium to unsafe levels.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid extra potassium unless your doctor approves.

interactions.message: Alcohol with metformin increases the risk of low blood sugar and lactic acidosis.
interactions.recommendation: Limit alcohol and discuss safe limits with your doctor.

interactions.message: Metformin may need to be paused around CT/angiography contrast dye to protect the kidneys.
interactions.recommendation: Tell the hospital team you take metformin before any scan with contrast.

interactions.message: Amlodipine can raise simvastatin levels and increase muscle pain risk.
interactions.recommendation: Report unexplained muscle pain to your doctor.

interactions.message: Atorvastatin with gemfibrozil raises the risk of serious muscle damage (rhabdomyolysis).
interactions.recommendation: This combination is usually avoided — call your doctor.

interactions.message: Ibuprofen may reduce aspirin's heart-protective effect and add stomach bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Ask your doctor before combining painkillers.

interactions.message: Alcohol with glimepiride can cause dangerous low blood sugar.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid drinking on an empty stomach while on this medicine.

interactions.message: Calcium can block levothyroxine absorption if taken together.
interactions.recommendation: Take thyroid medicine on an empty stomach and calcium at least 4 hours apart.

interactions.message: Amlodipine and telmisartan are often prescribed together for blood pressure — usually safe under doctor supervision.
interactions.recommendation: Watch for dizziness or swelling of feet. Do not stop without your doctor.

food_rules.message: Take paracetamol (Crocin) after food if it upsets your stomach.
Take metformin after meals to reduce stomach upset and diarrhoea.
Take pantoprazole (Pan-D) about 30 minutes before breakfast for best effect.
Atorvastatin is often taken in the evening when cholesterol production is higher.
Take levothyroxine on an empty stomach with water — wait 30–60 minutes before food or tea.
Take low-dose aspirin after food if it upsets your stomach.
Telmisartan (Telma) can be taken with or without food at the same time each day.
Amlodipine can be taken with or without food.
Take glimepiride shortly before meals to avoid low blood sugar.
Do not take with milk, curd, or calcium-rich foods — they reduce absorption.

effects: Nausea or stomach upset in some people
Liver harm if taken above the prescribed dose
Rare skin rash or allergic reaction
Long-term use may affect vitamin B12 absorption
Dizziness, especially when standing up quickly
Rare swelling of face or lips
Nausea, bloating, or loose stools when starting
Rare lactic acidosis — seek help for severe weakness or breathing trouble
Muscle aches or weakness — report to doctor
Ringing in ears at high doses

alternatives.name: Dolo 650
alternatives.note: Higher strength paracetamol — confirm dose with doctor

alternatives.name: Pantocid 40
alternatives.note: Same PPI class — only switch if doctor agrees

alternatives.name: Rabeprazole (Razo)
alternatives.note: Different PPI — not interchangeable without doctor advice

alternatives.name: Telvas 40
alternatives.note: Same ARB — confirm brand with pharmacist

alternatives.name: Losartan (Losar)
alternatives.note: Different ARB class — doctor must prescribe

alternatives.name: Gluformin
alternatives.note: Extended-release option — not same as plain metformin

alternatives.name: Clopivas 75
alternatives.note: Same anti-platelet — do not switch without cardiologist

alternatives.name: Aspirin (Ecosprin)
alternatives.note: Different drug — never substitute on your own
